Role/Contribution: Central figure of Christianity, crucified under Pontius Pilate
One of Jesus’ apostles, first Bishop of Rome (Pope)
Missionary, wrote Epistles, major figure in spreading Christianity
Apostle of Jesus, traditionally attributed to writing the Gospel of John and Revelation
Bishop of Antioch, wrote letters defining early church beliefs, martyred in Rome
Fourth Pope, wrote letters influencing church governance
Disciple of John, Bishop of Smyrna, martyred
Apologist, defended Christianity against pagan criticism
Bishop of Lyon, fought Gnostic heresies, wrote Against Heresies
Early scholar, wrote Hexapla, allegorical interpretation of scripture
First Antipope, wrote extensively on church order and heresies
Attempted to merge Christianity with Sol Invictus worship
Initiated one of the first systematic persecutions of Christians
Persecuted Christians, executed Pope Sixtus II and Saint Lawrence
Issued edicts of toleration for Christians
Bishop of Carthage, wrote on church unity, martyred under Valerian
Led the Great Persecution of Christians
